synthesis_text: COVID-19 is a contagious disease caused by the coronavirus SARS-CoV-2. It began spreading worldwide in January 2020, leading to a pandemic, and was declared a global health emergency by the World Health Organization in March 2020 [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]. Transmission occurs mainly through inhaling air contaminated by droplets or aerosols [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00024].

COVID-19 is an illness caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus, which emerged at the end of 2019 and became a pandemic in 2020. The virus spreads mainly through close contact and tiny droplets from coughs, sneezes, or talking. Many individuals with COVID-19 experience mild illness or no symptoms, but it can be severe for older adults and those with medical conditions. Complications from severe COVID-19 can include acute respiratory distress syndrome and damage to organs like the heart and brain. COVID-19 can lead to long-term symptoms, known as post-COVID-19 syndrome, which researchers are still studying. [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00000][doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007][doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00004][doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00015][doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00017]

COVID-19 is an infectious respiratory illness caused by a coronavirus called SARS-CoV-2. 'CO' stands for corona, 'VI' for virus, and 'D' for disease. Symptoms may appear 2 to 14 days after exposure to the virus and can range from very mild to severe illness. Some people who have been infected don’t have any symptoms. COVID-19 has been described as a 'pandemic', referring to its geographical spread.
